description: Phase 5d — Per-developer ML-KEM keypair crypto (replaces password-derived PBKDF2 wrapping) + comprehensive dashboard navigation fix. Unblocks OAuth dashboard users and MCP server users from creating encrypted projects, and adds three navigation paths back to the projects list from inside a project view. HARD CUTOVER: envelope-crypto.ts is deleted and any pre-existing PBKDF2-wrapped projects (e.g., 'TaskFlow App' demo) will lose access to searchable/private columns. This is acceptable per pre-production data policy (confirmed 2026-04-09).

userStories.title: Export ML-KEM primitives from the SDK
userStories.description: As a developer consuming @pqdb/client, I want generateKeyPair, encapsulate, and decapsulate to be exported from the package root so that the dashboard, MCP server, and any future client can use them without reaching into internal paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Write a failing unit test at sdk/tests/unit/pqc-export.test.ts that imports generateKeyPair, encapsulate, decapsulate from '@pqdb/client' and verifies a full round-trip (generate → encapsulate → decapsulate) produces matching shared secrets
sdk/src/index.ts adds the three named exports and their TypeScript types (KeyPair, EncapsulationResult)
No functional change to existing exports; sdk/dist/index.d.ts additions are purely additive
Unit tests pass
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
CI passes
userStories.notes: Merged via PR #143 (cbd845a). All 3 verification layers passed: CI 5/5 green, Semgrep 0 SAST, reviewers APPROVE x3, LSP semantic chain hits real @noble/post-quantum ml_kem768.

userStories.title: Backend migration — add ml_kem_public_key column to developers
userStories.description: As the backend, I want to store each developer's ML-KEM-768 public key so that I can return it to clients who need to verify/use it on project-scoped operations.
acceptanceCriteria: New alembic migration backend/alembic/versions/011_add_ml_kem_public_key_to_developers.py adds ml_kem_public_key BYTEA NULLABLE to the developers table
backend/src/pqdb_api/models/developer.py exposes the column as ml_kem_public_key: Mapped[bytes | None]
Migration applies cleanly against a fresh database
Migration is reversible — downgrade removes the column without errors
Existing developers rows have ml_kem_public_key=NULL after migration
Integration tests pass
Service responds to health check (/health returns 200)
Unit tests pass
Typecheck (mypy strict) passes
Production build succeeds
CI passes
userStories.notes: Merged via PR #145 (51273ecd). 5/5 CI green after one-line asyncio.run() fix in test_roles_policies.py (pre-existing Python 3.12 latent bug exposed by test ordering). 0 SAST, 3 reviewers APPROVE/CLEAN. LSP: migration chains 010→011 cleanly, downgrade is inverse, model field is mypy-strict Mapped[bytes | None].

userStories.title: Backend signup endpoint accepts and stores ml_kem_public_key
userStories.description: As a developer signing up, I want the signup endpoint to accept my ML-KEM-768 public key (generated client-side) and persist it on my developer record so that future project-creation calls can use it for wrapping.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /v1/auth/signup accepts an optional ml_kem_public_key: str field (base64-encoded) in the request body
On successful signup, the public key is stored in developers.ml_kem_public_key (decoded to bytes)
The existing response shape is unchanged ({access_token, refresh_token, token_type})
New endpoint GET /v1/auth/me/public-key returns {public_key: str | null} (base64) for logged-in developers — requires valid bearer token
Integration test at backend/tests/integration/test_auth_public_key.py: sign up WITH public key, verify it's stored, fetch via GET endpoint, bytes match the original
Integration test: sign up WITHOUT public key, stored as NULL, GET endpoint returns {public_key: null}
Integration test: GET /v1/auth/me/public-key without bearer token returns 401
Integration tests pass
Service responds to health check (/health returns 200)
Unit tests pass
Typecheck (mypy strict) passes
Production build succeeds
CI passes
userStories.notes: Merged via PR #146 (2a61e309) after 1 fix cycle. Original commit had missing length validation (silent failure: any base64 was accepted, including 0/100/2000-byte garbage). Fix added: ML_KEM_768_PUBLIC_KEY_BYTES=1184 constant + length check in _validate_b64; HTTPBearer(auto_error=False) to return 401 instead of FastAPI's default 403; 4 new tests (wrong-length, empty, oversized, two-developer isolation). Bonus: GET /v1/auth/me/public-key now returns 401 uniformly across all 60+ protected endpoints.

userStories.title: Dashboard signup flow generates and persists the ML-KEM keypair
userStories.description: As a dashboard user signing up, I want the dashboard to generate my ML-KEM-768 keypair, save the private key to IndexedDB, upload the public key to the server, and offer a recovery file download so that I can restore my key on a different device or in the MCP server.
acceptanceCriteria: dashboard/src/routes/signup.tsx calls generateKeyPair() before submitting the form
New file dashboard/src/lib/keypair-store.ts wraps IndexedDB under database 'pqdb' with store 'keypairs', keyed by developer id — exposes saveKeypair, loadKeypair, deleteKeypair
The private key is stored in IndexedDB under a key bound to the developer id (pqdb:keypair:{developer_id})
The public key is sent in the signup POST body as ml_kem_public_key (base64)
New component dashboard/src/components/recovery-file-modal.tsx shows after successful signup with a 'Download recovery file' button
The recovery file is pqdb-recovery-{email}.json containing {version: 1, developer_id, email, public_key, private_key, created_at, warning} (keys as base64)
Users cannot dismiss the recovery modal without either downloading the file OR checking 'I understand — I will not be able to decrypt my data if I lose this key'
Unit test: mocked IndexedDB and fetch — component generates a keypair on submit, verifies POST body contains a base64 public key, verifies IndexedDB write happens
E2E test (Playwright) at dashboard/e2e/signup-keypair.spec.ts: signup happy path downloads a parseable recovery file with both keys
Unit tests pass
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Verify in browser: signup flow generates keypair, shows modal, downloads file, navigates to /projects
CI passes
userStories.notes: Merged via PR #148 (62ba41c6) after 1 fix cycle. Original commit had no try/catch around handleSubmit AND wrong ordering (setTokens BEFORE saveKeypair). If saveKeypair threw on IDB quota / private mode, the user was authenticated with no private key — exactly the failure mode US-004 exists to prevent. Fix: reordered (saveKeypair → setTokens), added catch with tokensSet rollback flag, 3 new tests for the failure paths (generateKeyPair reject, saveKeypair reject, malformed JWT). Re-review CLEAN.

userStories.title: Dashboard keypair context — load from IndexedDB (happy path only)
userStories.description: As a dashboard user logging back in on a device that already has my keypair, I want the dashboard to load my private key from IndexedDB automatically and expose it via a React context so that downstream components (Create Project, project load) can use it without password prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: New file dashboard/src/lib/keypair-context.tsx replaces dashboard/src/lib/envelope-key-context.tsx and exposes a useKeypair() hook returning {publicKey, privateKey, loaded, error}
Context loads keypair from IndexedDB on login; if present, exposes it; if missing, sets error='missing' but does NOT render any modal yet (handled in US-005b)
All existing consumers of envelope-key-context are migrated to use keypair-context
When error='missing', the dashboard shows a simple placeholder banner 'Encryption key not loaded. Keypair recovery coming soon.' (replaced by the modal in US-005b)
Unit test: context loads from IndexedDB mock, returns {publicKey, privateKey, loaded: true} on happy path
Unit test: context with empty IndexedDB returns {loaded: true, error: 'missing'}
E2E test: existing happy path still works (keypair in IndexedDB → Create Project works)
Unit tests pass
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Verify in browser: fresh login with keypair in IndexedDB loads it transparently
CI passes
userStories.notes: Merged via PR #150 (caf70e42) after 1 fix cycle. Original had useEffect([]) race — after fresh login the keypair never loaded because the effect ran before the token existed and never retried. Fixed by adding onLogin() callback to auth-store (mirrors onLogout), tracking token in useState, depending on [token]. All 8 consumers migrated from envelope-key-context to keypair-context. E2E test added for signup→create project happy path.

userStories.title: Dashboard recovery modal — upload recovery file or regenerate keypair
userStories.description: As a dashboard user logging back in on a new device (or after clearing storage, or after OAuth signup), I want a modal that lets me either upload my recovery file to restore my keypair, or generate a new keypair (with a warning about old projects becoming unrecoverable).
acceptanceCriteria: New component dashboard/src/components/recover-keypair-modal.tsx renders when keypair-context reports error='missing'
Modal shows two options: 'Upload recovery file' and 'Generate new keypair'
Upload flow: file input, parses JSON, validates public key in file matches GET /v1/auth/me/public-key, stores private key in IndexedDB via keypair-store.ts, reloads keypair-context
Upload flow rejects mismatched public key with clear error: 'This recovery file does not match your account'
Regenerate flow: calls generateKeyPair(), PUTs new public key to new endpoint PUT /v1/auth/me/public-key, clears IndexedDB, stores new keypair, reloads keypair-context
Regenerate flow shows red warning: 'All projects created before this moment will become unrecoverable. This cannot be undone.' with an 'I understand' checkbox required before proceeding
Backend: PUT /v1/auth/me/public-key endpoint added for key rotation
Backend unit test: PUT endpoint requires auth, updates the column, returns 200
Frontend unit test: modal renders on error='missing', upload happy path, upload signature mismatch, regenerate happy path
E2E test: log out, clear IndexedDB, log back in → modal appears → upload valid recovery file → projects decrypt
E2E test: log out, clear IndexedDB, log back in → modal appears → regenerate → old projects show unrecoverable banner
Unit tests pass
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Verify in browser: both paths work, error messages clear, regenerate warning is prominent
CI passes

userStories.title: Dashboard Create Project uses ML-KEM encapsulate
userStories.description: As a dashboard user creating a project, I want the project encryption key to be produced via encapsulate(my_public_key) so that I never have to enter a password and the backend never sees the plaintext key.
acceptanceCriteria: dashboard/src/components/create-project-dialog.tsx no longer imports from envelope-crypto.ts — it imports encapsulate from '@pqdb/client'

Create project flow: calls encapsulate(publicKey) → {ciphertext, sharedSecret}, sends base64(ciphertext) as wrapped_encryption_key in POST /v1/projects body, stores sharedSecret in the keypair context under the new project id
dashboard/src/lib/projects.ts createProject() signature is unchanged (still takes wrappedEncryptionKey as base64 string)
After successful creation, the dialog closes and navigates to the new project's overview with the per-session encryption key available for row operations
dashboard/src/lib/envelope-crypto.ts is DELETED (dead code after this change)
Any remaining references to envelope-crypto are removed
Unit test: dialog calls encapsulate (mocked from '@pqdb/client'), POSTs correct body with base64 ciphertext, stores sharedSecret in context
E2E test: create a project, create a searchable-column table, insert a row, query it back → row decrypts to original plaintext
Unit tests pass
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Verify in browser: create project, no password prompt, wrapped_encryption_key is not null in the project response
CI passes

userStories.title: Dashboard project-load flow unwraps via decapsulate
userStories.description: As a dashboard user opening a project I created earlier, I want the dashboard to decapsulate the stored ciphertext with my private key to recover the project encryption key, transparently, without prompting me for a password.
acceptanceCriteria: On navigating to /projects/{id}, the route loader fetches the project detail, reads wrapped_encryption_key, calls decapsulate(ciphertext, privateKey) to recover the shared secret, stores it in the keypair context keyed by project id
Row operations (select, insert, update, delete) on sensitive columns use this recovered shared secret as the project encryption key
If wrapped_encryption_key is null, show an informational banner: 'This project has no encryption key configured. Sensitive columns will be unavailable.'
If decapsulate throws, show 'Could not decrypt this project. You may need to upload a different recovery file.'
Unit test: keypair context decrypts a mocked project, stores the recovered secret, retrieval returns the same secret for the same project id
E2E test: create a project, close the tab, reopen it → rows in searchable columns decrypt successfully
Unit tests pass
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Verify in browser: open an existing project, no password prompt, searchable columns render decrypted values
CI passes

userStories.title: MCP server supports PQDB_PRIVATE_KEY and uses encapsulate in create_project
userStories.description: As a user running the MCP server, I want to pass my ML-KEM private key via PQDB_PRIVATE_KEY so that the MCP can create projects with proper encryption and perform CRUD on searchable/private columns.
followUps: nl-query.ts generated filters from NL parsing don't go through transformFilters yet — NL .eq on a searchable column will miss
MCP tests are not in CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml has no mcp job) — structural gap that allowed broken tests to land in attempts 1-2
Error message in crud-tools.ts 'no key' path only mentions PQDB_ENCRYPTION_KEY and OAuth, should also mention PQDB_PRIVATE_KEY + pqdb_select_project
acceptanceCriteria: mcp/src/config.ts reads an optional PQDB_PRIVATE_KEY env var (base64 or base64url encoded)
Config decodes the key to Uint8Array and verifies the length matches the expected ML-KEM-768 private key size (~2400 bytes); fails fast with a clear error on mismatch
mcp/src/auth-state.ts exposes setCurrentPrivateKey(key: Uint8Array) and getCurrentPrivateKey()

pqdb_create_project with PQDB_PRIVATE_KEY set: fetches developer public key via GET /v1/auth/me/public-key, calls encapsulate(publicKey) to get (ciphertext, sharedSecret), sends base64(ciphertext) as wrapped_encryption_key in the POST body, stores sharedSecret as the active encryption key — does NOT leak the raw key in the tool response

pqdb_create_project WITHOUT PQDB_PRIVATE_KEY: creates a plaintext-only project as today, emits a warning in the tool result: 'No PQDB_PRIVATE_KEY set — project created without encryption. Set PQDB_PRIVATE_KEY to enable searchable/private columns.'
pqdb_select_project fetches the project, decapsulates wrapped_encryption_key with the configured private key, makes the recovered shared secret available for subsequent CRUD
Unit test: mocked encapsulate/decapsulate, verify pqdb_create_project sends the correct body and stores the shared secret in auth-state
Unit test: pqdb_create_project without PQDB_PRIVATE_KEY creates plaintext project and returns warning

Integration test: boot the real MCP server with a generated private key, create a project, assert wrapped_encryption_key != null in the tool result, run a round-trip insert/query on a searchable column
Integration tests pass
Service responds to health check (MCP server responds on /mcp endpoint)
Unit tests pass
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
CI passes
userStories.notes: Merged via PR #149 (ed034ebc) after 3 fix cycles. Attempt 1: shared secret stored but never consumed by CRUD — encryption_active=true was unfalsifiable. Attempt 2: wired getCurrentEncryptionKeyString into getCryptoState but pqdb_query_rows never called transformFilters — integration test fails at line 365 with 0 rows (HMAC digest vs plaintext mismatch). Agent didn't run the test. Attempt 3: wired transformFilters in query/update/delete + nl-query.ts dynamic key. Agent ran integration test locally, 3/3 pass. Reviewer ran it independently, confirmed 3/3 pass.

userStories.title: Dashboard navigation back to projects list — comprehensive fix
userStories.description: As a dashboard user inside a project, I want at least three ways to navigate back to the projects list (clickable logo, 'All Projects' in the project switcher dropdown, clickable breadcrumb) so that I can always find my way back regardless of which UI convention I'm used to.
followUps: Breadcrumb shows raw project UUID briefly while useQuery resolves / silently on fetch error — add a loading skeleton and an error state (toast or '(name unavailable)' label). Navigation still works because the Link's `to` uses activeProjectId, but the UX is rough.
Add unit tests for top-bar breadcrumb pending and error states (gap noted by silent-failure-hunter on PR #144).
Remove dead page.goto(/projects/{id}/schema) in nav-back-to-projects.spec.ts test #5 — selector is only rendered outside a project by design, so the intermediate navigation asserts nothing.
acceptanceCriteria: dashboard/src/components/sidebar-nav.tsx wraps the 'pqdb' logo span in a <Link to="/projects"> — hovering shows cursor-pointer, clicking navigates to /projects

dashboard/src/components/project-selector.tsx (or equivalent) adds 'All Projects' as the first item in the Select project popover, separated from the project list by a divider — clicking navigates to /projects

dashboard/src/components/top-bar.tsx changes the breadcrumb from 'Account / Select project / main' to 'Account / All projects / {ProjectName} / {BranchName}' when inside a project — 'All projects' is a clickable Link to /projects, {ProjectName} is a clickable Link to /projects/{id}
All three navigation paths preserve the current theme, session, and sidebar scroll position
Unit test: sidebar-nav renders logo as Link with correct href
Unit test: project-selector renders 'All Projects' as first item with correct href
Unit test: top-bar renders breadcrumb with 'All projects' as clickable link when on /projects/{id} routes
E2E test (Playwright): from /projects/{id}/schema, click the pqdb logo → lands on /projects
E2E test: from /projects/{id}/schema, click 'All Projects' in the dropdown → lands on /projects
E2E test: from /projects/{id}/schema, click the 'All projects' breadcrumb → lands on /projects
Unit tests pass
Typecheck passes
Production build succeeds
Verify in browser: all three paths work interactively
CI passes
userStories.notes: Merged via PR #144 (3c451990). 5/5 CI green, 0 SAST, 2 reviewers APPROVE + 1 non-blocking silent-failure finding tracked in followUps. LSP: all 3 nav paths resolve to real @tanstack/react-router Links.
